concepts.description: The ball's lateral position normalized against the pitch centerline and touchline distance.

implementation.description: Current Python implementation registered in the runtime catalog.

operationalizations.description: Normalize ball distance from pitch centerline toward either touchline.

limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
runtime_contract.name: ball_lateral_fraction
runtime_contract.purpose: Normalize ball distance from pitch centerline toward either touchline.

known_deviations: Control candidates are currently collapsed into final status rather than retained as a candidate distribution.

concepts.description: The ball moves from one player to a teammate and the teammate gains controlled possession.

operationalizations.description: Use event data to find candidate completed passes, then tracking data to confirm physical release and controlled reception.

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.
runtime_contract.name: controlled_pass_episode

parameters.description: Search window before the event anchor for passer control.
parameters.name: release_search_before_seconds

parameters.description: Search window after the event anchor for ball departure.
parameters.name: release_search_after_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum release-to-controlled-reception search window.
parameters.name: reception_search_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum ball-player distance for control evidence.
parameters.name: control_distance_m

parameters.description: Margin allowing the intended player to count as controlling near teammates.
parameters.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

parameters.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
parameters.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

runtime_contract.purpose: Identify completed pass actions where event data supplies the candidate passer/receiver and tracking proves physical release plus controlled reception.

known_deviations: Active-player membership uses current canonical outfield metadata and observed frame positions rather than the standalone authoritative active-player timeline.

concepts.description: A compact band of defending outfield players goal-side of the ball under declared geometric thresholds.

operationalizations.description: Fit a compact goal-side band of defending outfield players at supplied anchor frames.

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
runtime_contract.name: defensive_line_model

parameters.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
parameters.name: line_band_width_m

parameters.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line.
parameters.name: minimum_line_defenders

runtime_contract.purpose: Fit an observed geometric defensive line at supplied anchor frames from defending outfield player tracking positions.

known_deviations: Active-player membership is derived from current implementation behavior rather than a standalone authoritative active-player timeline.

concepts.description: The aggregate position of defending outfield players, excluding the goalkeeper.

implementation.description: Current Python implementation registered in the runtime catalog.

operationalizations.description: Measure defending outfield team centroid without goalkeeper bias.

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.
runtime_contract.name: defensive_outfield_centroid
runtime_contract.purpose: Measure defending outfield team centroid without goalkeeper bias.

known_deviations: Reviewed-plan-specific classifier is intentionally not a general-purpose authorable primitive.

concepts.description: A saved-plan classification rule whose assumptions are controlled by a reviewed tactical recipe.

operationalizations.description: Classify post-anchor outcome under reviewed block-shift recipe semantics.

limitations: Does not infer intent, optimality, or missed opportunity.
runtime_contract.name: outcome_classification

parameters.description: Outcome lookahead horizon in seconds.
parameters.name: horizon

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify the post-anchor outcome under the frozen M1 predicates.

concepts.description: Active-ball possession intervals and anchors for a perspective team.

implementation.description: Current Python implementation registered in the runtime catalog.

operationalizations.description: Identify active-ball possession intervals and anchors for the perspective team.

runtime_contract.name: possession_segment
runtime_contract.purpose: Identify active-ball possession intervals for the perspective team.

concepts.description: Ball entry into the destination region defined by a relation episode within a declared window.

implementation.description: Current generic relation destination entry implementation.

operationalizations.description: Measure whether the ball enters a relation episode destination region inside a declared window.

limitations: Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
runtime_contract.name: relation_destination_entry

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic witness ordering when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

runtime_contract.purpose: Measure whether the ball enters the exact destination region of a relation episode inside a declared post-open window.

known_deviations: Reviewed-plan-specific wrapper; AI-authored plans should use relation_destination_entry.

concepts.description: Ball entry into the destination region defined by a relation episode within a declared window.

exposure_policy.description: Reviewed-plan-only destination entry classifier exposure policy.

implementation.display_name: Relation Destination Entry Classification Python Implementation

operationalizations.description: Classify ball entry into a relation destination for a reviewed saved-plan path.

limitations: Trusted recipe wrapper; agent-authored plans should use relation_destination_entry.
Requires an upstream relation episode set.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
runtime_contract.name: relation_destination_entry_classification

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for destination lane entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic experimental result IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic selection strategy when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify whether the ball later enters the destination region of a previously evaluated relation episode.

known_deviations: Entity identity and evaluation frame are selected from bounded anchor-record fields rather than a standalone semantic entity selector.

concepts.description: An entity's observed position classified ahead of, behind, or level with a supplied geometric line.

implementation.display_name: Relative Position To Line Python Implementation

operationalizations.description: Classify an entity's tracking position relative to a supplied observed line at a declared frame.

limitations: Classifies only observed position relative to a supplied geometric line.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
runtime_contract.name: relative_position_to_line

parameters.description: Entity-reference field read from the entity anchor record.
parameters.name: entity_id_field

parameters.description: Frame field at which the entity position is evaluated.
parameters.name: entity_frame_field

parameters.description: Distance from the line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify an entity position as ahead of, behind, or level with an observed line at a declared frame.

concepts.description: Baseline-relative lateral movement of a defending outfield block toward the ball side.

operationalizations.description: Measure defensive centroid movement toward the ball side relative to a baseline.

runtime_contract.name: signed_lateral_shift
runtime_contract.purpose: Measure defensive centroid movement toward the ball side.

concepts.description: A geometrically clear forward connection from the ball or anchor to a teammate.

operationalizations.description: Represent a clear forward connection from ball location to attacking teammate over an interval.

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No receiver body-orientation model.
No offside model in V1 acceptance.
runtime_contract.name: geometric_progressive_corridor

known_deviations: Active-opponent denominator is implemented for current data shape but not yet backed by a standalone authoritative active-player timeline.

concepts.description: Count of opposition outfield players whose position relative to the ball changes between action endpoints.

implementation.description: Current release-to-reception opponent transition count implementation.
implementation.display_name: Opponents Bypassed By Action Python Implementation

operationalizations.description: Count opposition outfield players goal-side of the ball at release and behind the ball at controlled reception.

limitations: Counts relative ball-position change between release and controlled reception.
Does not prove the pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
Goalkeepers are excluded from the opponent denominator.
runtime_contract.name: opponents_bypassed_by_action

parameters.description: Release-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is goal-side of the ball.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Reception-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is behind the ball.
parameters.name: bypassed_buffer_m

runtime_contract.purpose: Count opposition outfield players who were goal-side of the ball at action release and behind the ball at controlled reception.
